thread.title: Agent Memory
thread.definitionDraft: Agent Memory refers to systems and architectures that enable AI agents to preserve, manage, and recall information—such as working state, user preferences, project-specific facts, and external knowledge—across multiple reasoning steps, tool calls, or interactive sessions. It is distinct from simply expanding the model's context window or using retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), focusing instead on structured, persistent, and often self-managed memory stores.
thread.whyNow: As AI agents are deployed for more complex, long-running, and personalized tasks (e.g., continuous coding assistance, long-term project management, personalized tutoring), the limitations of transient context and stateless interactions become critical bottlenecks. Robust memory systems are essential for agent reliability, personalization, and task continuity, driving rapid innovation in this space.
thread.useBoundary: This topic covers systems for state persistence, memory management, and knowledge retention across agent steps/sessions. It excludes discussions focused solely on increasing the base model's context length or basic RAG pipelines without explicit memory layer abstractions.

sourceRequirements.companyStrategyContextPolicy: Company strategy sources may be kept only as optional company_strategy_context targets and must not count toward topic readiness.

sources.title: A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents (Section on Memory)
sources.whyRelevant: Comprehensive survey paper that includes a detailed taxonomy and analysis of memory mechanisms in LLM-based agents.
sources.evidenceQuote: Memory mechanisms enable agents to store and retrieve past experiences and knowledge
sources.reviewNotes: Academic survey providing a broad overview of the field.
sources.text:
A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents (Section on Memory)

Comprehensive survey paper that includes a detailed taxonomy and analysis of memory mechanisms in LLM-based agents.

Evidence note: Memory mechanisms enable agents to store and retrieve past experiences and knowledge

Academic survey providing a broad overview of the field.

edges.evidenceNote: The mem0 documentation describes the architecture, and the GitHub repository contains its primary implementation.
Letta's documentation provides the conceptual overview, and its GitHub repo is the main source code.
LangGraph is built on LangChain concepts and represents a more advanced, graph-based approach to including memory in agent workflows.
Lilian Weng's influential blog post discusses and contextualizes memory research, including the MemGPT paper's contributions.
The survey paper on LLM-based agents cites and discusses the MemGPT paper as part of the memory mechanism analysis.

review.notes: First-pass Mimo pack after URL audit. Dead or placeholder URLs were removed; keep source_pack_review until human content audit passes.
